medusa_blood.name: Medusa's Blood
medusa_blood.lore: The ichor of the Gorgon. From her right side flows poison; from her left, a healing draught.

dragon_scale.name: Dragon Scale
dragon_scale.lore: A gleaming scale of fireproof dragon hide. It smells of sulfur and ancient gold.

wild_thyme.name: wild thyme
wild_thyme.lore: A hardy aromatic herb that grows in the cracks of dungeon walls. Cooks prize it as a versatile flavoring that subtly sharpens the senses.

rosemary_sprig.name: rosemary sprig
rosemary_sprig.lore: A woody-stemmed herb whose resinous scent has been known to ward off minor hexes. Adventurers crush it in their palms before entering cursed chambers.

valerian_root.name: valerian root
valerian_root.lore: A gnarled root with sedative properties. Herbalists dry and powder it to calm nerves; master cooks transmute it into a potent focus-enhancing tonic.

nightshade_leaf.name: nightshade leaf
nightshade_leaf.lore: A deep-violet leaf from a poisonous plant. In small, precisely cooked doses it sharpens reflexes to a razor edge—but miscalculate and the result is lethal.

mandrake_root.name: mandrake root
mandrake_root.lore: A humanoid-shaped root infamous for its screech when pulled. Ground and slow-roasted by an expert cook, it imparts a formidable boost to raw physical power.

cave_mushroom.name: cave mushroom
cave_mushroom.lore: A pale, plump mushroom grown in the darkness of deep caverns. Bland on its own, but a skilled cook can render it into a surprisingly nourishing meal.

pale_celery.name: pale celery
pale_celery.lore: Etiolated celery stalks that grow in underground streams. Water-rich and faintly bitter, they form the base of many dungeon soups.

cave_carrot.name: cave carrot
cave_carrot.lore: Orange roots that somehow flourish in dimly lit passages. Rich in subterranean minerals, they are said to grant keen eyesight in the dark.

glowing_spore.name: glowing spore
glowing_spore.lore: A bioluminescent spore cluster that pulses with soft blue-green light. Ingesting it in a refined preparation briefly heightens magical sensitivity.

crimson_fungus.name: crimson fungus
crimson_fungus.lore: Blood-red caps with a fiery bite. Herbalists warn never to eat these raw—the toxins must be rendered harmless through careful cooking before the energizing compounds can be safely absorbed.

petrified_mushroom.name: petrified mushroom
petrified_mushroom.lore: Ancient fungi turned to near-stone over centuries of mineral exposure. Ground to a fine dust and boiled, they yield a broth of remarkable fortifying properties.

ember_spice.name: ember spice
ember_spice.lore: Tiny flakes of solidified volcanic mineral, glowing faintly orange. Sprinkled into a dish they intensify flavors and imbue a transient heat-resistance.

serpent_pepper.name: serpent pepper
serpent_pepper.lore: A slender, venomously-hot pepper found near serpent dens. Cooking neutralizes its toxins and leaves behind a compound that sharpens dexterity for hours.

void_salt.name: void salt
void_salt.lore: Crystallized residue from extra-planar rifts. A grain transforms any dish into something otherworldly—and grants brief resistance to necrotic energies.

dragon_salt.name: dragon salt
dragon_salt.lore: Crystalline deposits found only in dragon lairs, formed when draconic flame meets limestone. Chefs who master its use produce meals of legendary restorative power.

minotaur_horn.name: Minotaur Horn
minotaur_horn.lore: A massive curved horn from the Minotaur of Knossos. Radiates primal fury.

fenrir_fang.name: Fenrir's Fang
fenrir_fang.lore: A colossal tooth from the World-Wolf, still crackling with cold fury.

void_essence.name: Void Essence
void_essence.lore: Crystallized nothingness scraped from the Destroyer's form. It absorbs light and hums with terrible potential.

5.name: lord of the dark sky banquet

prime_beef.name: prime beef
prime_beef.lore: A thick cut of marbled beef from a Hell Bovine. Despite its infernal origins, it smells absolutely delicious. Moo.

royal_beef.name: royal beef
royal_beef.lore: An exquisite cut of beef from the Cow King himself. The meat shimmers with a faint golden hue. Fit for royalty. Moo.
